prerequisites: An active Invoiced account with access to the dashboard at invoiced.com.
A valid API key generated under Settings -> Developers -> API Keys.
Decide which environment to use: production (https://api.invoiced.com) or sandbox (https://api.sandbox.invoiced.com).

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with the API key from the connector's api_key field. Invoiced uses HTTP Basic authentication, passing the API key as the username with an empty password (curl -u {YOUR_API_KEY}:). A valid API key is required for every request.

setupSteps.title: Generate an Invoiced API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Invoiced dashboard and go to Settings -> Developers -> API Keys. Create a key and copy its value. Keep it secret, since it grants access to your Invoiced account.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key into the required api_key field. The connector sends it as the HTTP Basic auth username (with an empty password) on each request to the Invoiced API.

setupSteps.title: Verify connectivity
setupSteps.body: Confirm requests succeed against the Invoiced API (production base URL https://api.invoiced.com, or the sandbox base URL https://api.sandbox.invoiced.com for testing). A 429 response indicates the rate limit was exceeded; retry after slowing down.
